points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights
points.text: TVK's policy is that true social justice means every community receives proportional representation in the appropriate measure. To ensure this, a Caste Survey will be conducted.
points.title: 1 Proportional Representation

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights
points.text: A resolution will be passed in the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly demanding the fair and full financial share due to Tamil Nadu from the taxes it contributes to the Union Government. Not stopping with merely passing the resolution, a Special Legal Committee will be constituted to take the fight up to the Supreme Court and legally argue for Tamil Nadu's financial share. Every rupee of revenue that should come to Tamil Nadu will be properly documented, and a report on the Union Government's injustice regarding Tamil Nadu's finances will be published. Furthermore, obtaining the fair financial share due to Tamil Nadu from the Union Government will be ensured.
points.title: 2 Demanding a Fair Share of Finances for Tamil Nadu

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 2 Demanding a Fair Share of Finances for Tamil Nadu
A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights
points.text: The TVK government will stand firm in securing Tamil Nadu's finances from the Union Government. In particular, it will strongly insist that 70 percent of the total GST tax revenue be given to the states. A proper GST compensation amount will also be demanded so that states are relieved from revenue losses.
points.title: 3 Rightful and Fair Share for Tamil Nadu from GST Tax Revenue

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 3 Rightful and Fair Share for Tamil Nadu from GST Tax Revenue
A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights
points.text: It is because education (including medical education) is on the Union Government's common list that impositions such as the new education policy and the NEET exam controversy cause educational obstacles. Therefore, appropriate steps will be taken to move education (including medical education) from the Union Government's common list to the state list. In this, appropriate measures will be taken to bring a constitutional amendment in Parliament in coordination with like-minded parties. A legal battle will also be pursued by filing a review petition in the Supreme Court on this issue.
points.title: 4 Appropriate Action to Move Education to the State List!

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 4 Appropriate Action to Move Education to the State List!
A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights
points.text: TVK strongly opposes the redrawing of parliamentary constituencies on the basis of population. To prevent South Indian states from being severely affected by delimitation, appropriate steps will be taken to bring a constitutional amendment.
points.title: 5 Protecting Tamil Nadu's Rights in Constituency Delimitation

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 5 Protecting Tamil Nadu's Rights in Constituency Delimitation
A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights
points.text: TVK strongly opposes the Union Government's 'One Nation, One Election' plan, which weakens state autonomy and the federal principle. Therefore, appropriate efforts will be made to have the One Nation One Election draft bill withdrawn.
points.title: 6 Opposition to One Nation One Election

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 6 Opposition to One Nation One Election
A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights
points.text: The three-language education scheme which the Union Government is attempting to impose through the new education policy will severely harm Tamil Nadu's linguistic heritage and the quality of education. On behalf of TVK, a resolution will be passed in the Legislative Assembly opposing the Union Government's new education policy. A special inter-state committee will be formed to work jointly with other state governments that share this view. It will be urged that all states in India implement a two-language policy with their respective mother tongue and English as the link language.
points.title: 7 Safeguarding the Two-Language Policy

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights
points.text: On behalf of TVK, continued pressure will be placed on the Union Government to abolish the NEET exam.
points.title: 8 Abolition of NEET Exam

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights
points.text: It will be insisted that the respective state language be used as the language of the court in High Courts.
points.title: 9 State Language in High Courts

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights
points.text: On behalf of TVK, continued pressure will be placed on the Union Government to establish a bench of the Supreme Court in Chennai.
points.title: 10 Supreme Court Bench in Chennai

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights
points.text: TVK will secure Tamil Nadu's rightful share of Cauvery water as per the 2018 Supreme Court verdict. Immediate legal action will be taken against any obstacles created in obtaining Tamil Nadu's share of water. Measures will be taken to further strengthen Tamil Nadu's rights in the Cauvery Water Management Authority. Through continuous and systematic monitoring, we will not yield even a single drop of Tamil Nadu's rightful share of Cauvery water!
points.title: 11 Cauvery River Water Rights

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights
points.text: In line with the Katchatheevu agreement made between the governments of India and Sri Lanka, TVK will sincerely and emphatically pressure the Union Government to take appropriate measures to reclaim and preserve the traditional fishing rights of Indian and Tamil Nadu fishermen in the Gulf of Mannar. It will be strongly urged with the Union Government to completely prevent Tamil Nadu fishermen from being arrested by the Sri Lankan Navy.
points.title: 12 Katchatheevu Fishing Rights

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights
points.text: On behalf of TVK, the Union Government will be urged to grant Indian citizenship to Sri Lankan Tamils who have migrated from Sri Lanka and are living in Tamil Nadu. Through this measure, it will be ensured that Sri Lankan Tamils receive all welfare schemes of the state government. Similarly, TVK will pressure the Union Government to take appropriate steps to protect the rights, cultural identity and possessions of Tamils living in Sri Lanka. TVK will continue to make appropriate efforts through the Union Government to eliminate ethnic discrimination prevailing in Sri Lanka.
points.title: 13 Justice for Sri Lankan Tamils

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights
points.text: TVK will continuously insist on abolishing the position of Governor, which continues as a vestige of colonial British India. Arrangements will be made for the state government itself to appoint Vice-Chancellors of universities.
points.title: 14 Action to Abolish Governor Appointments

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Finances and State Rights

points.text: To proclaim and protect the history of Tamils, who possess a unique heritage and pride in world history, Tamil cultural sites will be restored at a cost of Rs. 500 crore. Traditional Tamil cultural arts including silambam, kummi, kolattam, and Thanjavur painting will be revived. International-level cultural exhibitions will be held to make the historical pride and cultural identities of Tamils famous worldwide.
points.title: 1 Budget for Tamil Heritage and Civilization

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 1 Budget for Tamil Heritage and Civilization

points.text: TVK will support the Archaeology Department in continuing excavations at 10 sites: Keezhadi, Adichanallur, Kodumanal, Mayiladumparai, Sivakalai, Azhagankulam, Arikamedu, Saluvankuppam, Kaveripattinam, and Poompuhar. Efforts will be made to obtain UNESCO World Heritage Site recognition for both Keezhadi and Adichanallur. To help future generations learn our history, school student tours to these sites will be organised at government expense.
points.title: 2 Archaeological Excavations

points.text: To proclaim the thousands of years of Tamil history, an international-class 'Tamil Civilization Museum' will be established in Madurai.
points.title: 3 'Tamil Civilization Museum' in Madurai

points.text: Folk Artists Livelihood Protection Scheme: To ensure that registered folk artists get 100 days of employment per year, opportunities to showcase their artistic skills will be created through government functions and events. Additionally, financial assistance of Rs. 15,000 will be provided to help them buy the necessary instruments.
points.title: 4 Folk Artist Welfare Improvement

points.text: Young Artists Encouragement Scheme: To encourage emerging young folk artists, a grant of Rs. 25,000 per year will be given to 1,000 young artists.
points.title: Young Artists Encouragement Scheme

points.text: Folk arts research and training centres will be established in Coimbatore (karagattam, kummi, oyilattam), Thanjavur (Bharatanatyam, therukoothu, parai), and Tirunelveli (silambam, kambattam), where free training will be provided to interested students.
points.title: 5 Folk Arts Research and Training Centres

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 5 Folk Arts Research and Training Centres

points.text: The following schemes will be implemented to revive, encourage and develop Tamil Nadu's traditional sports: *A Tamil Nadu Rural Olympics series will be held to take Tamil traditional sports to the global stage. *Rs. 5 lakh financial assistance will be given by the government to conduct jallikattu events. Rs. 10 lakh will be given to the family of a bull-tamer who dies during jallikattu. Similarly, Rs. 2 lakh will be given to an injured participant. Further, measures will be taken to promote jallikattu as an international sport. *Rekla racing grounds will be built in districts such as Coimbatore and Salem where rekla races are predominantly held. *Rs. 5 crore will be provided annually to conduct silambam competitions at the international level. One thousand silambam training centres will be established across Tamil Nadu.
points.title: 6 Traditional Sports Development Scheme

points.text: A special scheme will be introduced with the aim of making Thirukkural a functional part of every student's life. Students who fully learn 300 kurals will be given cash prizes and educational scholarships; those who fully learn all 1,330 kurals will be given global cultural experience, state-level recognition and long-term educational support. Further, to build leadership rooted in Tamil heritage values, Thirukkural will be introduced as a special subject in schools and business education institutions.
points.title: 7 Thirukkural Development Scheme

points.text: To honour the sacrifice of the language struggle martyrs, the annual pension given to their families will be increased to Rs. 2,50,000 each. To remember the language struggle martyrs, speech and essay competitions will be held for students.
points.title: 8 Pensions for Language Struggle Martyrs

points.text: With the arrival of AI technology, every field has adapted accordingly. However, AI technology is based largely on English. Designing and using this technology in Tamil has become a necessity of the times. Therefore, a dedicated Tamil AI model will be developed in partnership with leading universities. This service will be provided free of charge to all our country's people. It will be provided to foreigners on the basis of a commercial licence.
points.title: 9 Tamil Artificial Intelligence

points.text: Sufficient funds will be allocated to create digital archives to protect Tamil Nadu's art, culture and heritage. Under the Tamil Nadu Digital Museum project, efforts will be made to document Tamil Nadu's history, culture and heritage through AR (Augmented Reality), VR (Virtual Reality) and Hologram technology.
points.title: 10 Digital Archives of Tamil Heritage

points.text: TVK's 'Tamil Nadu Responsible Tourism' is presented as a joyful and creative tourism scheme that treats tourism not merely as entertainment but includes eco-tourism, heritage tourism, coastal tourism and cultural tourism. 25 percent of the revenue from this will be spent on improving the livelihoods of the respective local people.
points.title: 1 Tamil Nadu Responsible Tourism

points.text: The following initiatives will be implemented to attract tourists by centering Tamil culture: Folk art performances will be held every evening at 10 major tourist sites. Rural tourism centres providing a village life experience will be created. Night markets selling handicrafts made by local artisans will be set up near all cultural tourism sites. A Tamil Cultural Festival will be held during tourist seasons including the summer holidays.
points.title: 2 Archaeological Excavations

points.text: A Tamil Nadu Spiritual Tourism scheme will be developed along four major spiritual routes: *The Arupadai Veedu Tourism Scheme covering the spiritual sites of Palani, Thirupparamkunram, Thiruchendur, Swamimalai, Thiruthani and Pazhamudircholai. *The Shaivite Temples Tourism Scheme covering Chidambaram, Tiruvannamalai, Kumbakonam and Thiruvaiyaru. *The Vaishnavite Holy Sites Tourism Scheme covering Srirangam, Thirukoshtiyur and Alwar Thirunagari. *The Inter-faith Harmony Tourism Scheme covering holy sites of all our religions, including Nagore Dargah, Velankanni and Kanyakumari.
points.title: 3 Spiritual Tourism Scheme

points.text: Rest houses for pilgrims will be built at Tiruvannamalai, Madurai, Rameswaram, Palani, Velankanni and Nagore — major spiritual worship centres for all religions.
points.title: 4 Rest Houses for Pilgrims

points.text: At cultural sites such as Keezhadi, Adichanallur, Kodumanal and Gangaikondacholapuram, the Tamil urban civilisation experience will be recreated for tourists through AR (Augmented Reality) and VR (Virtual Reality).
points.title: 5 Tamil Cultural Tourism Scheme

points.text: Tax concessions will be given to private owners who convert Chettinad houses, Nilgiri bungalows and similar heritage properties reflecting Tamil culture and tradition into guest houses. Additionally, the government will provide a 50 percent subsidy towards the cost of restoring such heritage buildings.
points.title: 6 Tamil Heritage Stays

points.text: A coastal cruise tourism route will be created along Tamil Nadu's long coastline from Vedaranyam to Rameswaram-Mandapam. The respective local fishermen themselves will serve as tour guides in their areas and as mariners taking tourists out to sea. Through this, their revenue loss during the fishing ban period will be prevented. Rameswaram-Dhanushkodi will be declared as Tamil Nadu's first coastal tourism district. Efforts will be made to start a boat service between Rameswaram and Talaimannar in Sri Lanka.
points.title: 7 Tamil Nadu Coastal Cruise Tourism

points.text: 15 trekking trails will be identified at Ooty, Coonoor, Gudalur, Kotagiri and Mudumalai, and the Nilgiri Mountain Adventure Tourism Scheme will be implemented. A Nilgiris Mountain Biking Festival will be held every year in an eco-friendly manner. In a government–private partnership, paragliding stations will be set up at three locations in Ooty. Facilities for kayaking on the Moyar river will be arranged.
points.title: 8 Nilgiri Mountain Adventure Tourism

points.text: Tamil Nadu's first caravan tourism policy will be created. Under this, beautiful landscapes across 20 tourism zones throughout Tamil Nadu will be selected, and caravan stops with drinking water, internet, electricity connections and toilet facilities will be set up. Entrepreneurs and self-help groups coming forward to set up these caravan stops will be given Rs. 2 lakh financial assistance.
points.title: 9 Caravan Tourism

points.text: To taste the traditional cuisines of Tamil Nadu, the Chettinad Food Tourism Trail (Karaikudi, Kanadukathan, Devakottai), Kongu Food Tourism Trail (Coimbatore, Erode, Salem) and Tirunelveli Food Tourism Trail will be created. An international food festival will be held to showcase our state's proud food culture.
points.title: 10 Food Tourism Trails

points.text: River-based tourism will be developed along three major river routes: the Cauvery river route from Hogenakkal Falls to Srirangam island, the Madurai Vaigai route, and the Tamirabarani river route from Papanasam to Tirunelveli.
points.title: 11 River-based Tourism

points.text: Twelve-week training courses covering hospitality, food preparation, tour guiding, water sports safety management and art skill development will be introduced. Employment will be guaranteed to those who complete these courses successfully.
points.title: 12 Tourism Skill Development Scheme

points.text: A cinema tourism trail covering famous shooting locations throughout Tamil Nadu will be created. Arrangements will be made for visitors to learn about the connections between these places and their respective films through QR codes.
points.title: 13 Cinema Tourism

points.text: To enhance safety features for tourists, infrastructure and basic facilities at tourist sites will be improved. In particular, qualified operators, coastal lifeguards, tourist police and 24-hour help centres will be provided.
points.title: 14 Tourism Safety

points.text: Creating a poverty-free Tamil Nadu is TVK's primary objective. Poor families in Tamil Nadu will be identified, and it will be ensured that all government welfare schemes reach them. Further, appropriate employment opportunities will be created for them. Opportunities for self-employment will also be created. Through these measures, their minimum annual income will be ensured.
points.title: 1 Poverty-Free Tamil Nadu

points.text: Eligible households will be given 200 units of free electricity every month.
points.title: 2 Free Electricity

points.text: 100 percent clean drinking water supply will be ensured to all homes in Tamil Nadu.
points.title: 3 Drinking Water for All

points.text: Five lakh new houses will be built so that poor and underprivileged people in rural areas can own homes.
points.title: 4 Housing for All

points.text: Government pattas will be issued to all those living on government poramboke lands (provided there are no objections). MGR patta documents currently held by people living in hill areas including Kalrayan will be properly computerised.
points.title: 5 Regularising Land Pattas

points.text: Priority will be given to the elderly, persons with disabilities and marginalised people by delivering ration items to their homes. To prevent anaemia and vitamin A and B-12 deficiencies among the public, iron and folic acid-enriched rice, vitamin A and vitamin D-enriched cooking oil, and fortified wheat flour will be distributed through ration shops to everyone. To prevent short-weighing, poor quality, adulteration and smuggling of ration items, all ration items other than rice will be distributed in packets, ensuring people get quality goods at fair weights. Strict quality checks will be conducted at every stage of the public distribution system. One ration shop will be established for every 500 ration cards. Sufficient weighers will be appointed in every rural ration shop.
points.title: 6 Public Distribution System Reform

points.subsection_title: B. Welfare of Adi Dravidars and Tribal Communities
points.text: The funds allocated in the budget for the welfare of Adi Dravidar-Tribal and marginalised communities will be used entirely for their development. This fund will never be used for other development schemes.
points.title: 1 Social Justice Budget

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Welfare of Adi Dravidars and Tribal Communities

points.subsection_title: B. Welfare of Adi Dravidars and Tribal Communities
points.text: Adi Dravidar and Tribal students pursuing professional education, medicine, law and management will be given a scholarship of Rs. 40,000 per year.
points.title: 2 Adi Dravidar Scholarship

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Welfare of Adi Dravidars and Tribal Communities

points.subsection_title: B. Welfare of Adi Dravidars and Tribal Communities
points.text: Under the Scheduled Caste Entrepreneur Scheme, first-generation entrepreneurs from Adi Dravidar and Tribal communities will be given Rs. 5 lakh as investment capital.
points.title: 3 Adi Dravidar Entrepreneur Scheme

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Welfare of Adi Dravidars and Tribal Communities

points.subsection_title: B. Welfare of Adi Dravidars and Tribal Communities
points.text: Loans taken by Adi Dravidar people from the Tamil Nadu Adi Dravidar Housing and Development Corporation will be fully waived.
points.title: 4 TAHDCO Loan Waiver

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Welfare of Adi Dravidars and Tribal Communities

points.subsection_title: B. Welfare of Adi Dravidars and Tribal Communities
points.text: Permanent housing and pattas will be provided to all Adi Dravidar and Tribal people in Tamil Nadu.
points.title: 5 Adi Dravidar and Tribal Housing Scheme

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 5 Adi Dravidar and Tribal Housing Scheme
B. Welfare of Adi Dravidars and Tribal Communities

points.subsection_title: B. Welfare of Adi Dravidars and Tribal Communities
points.text: Atrocities against Adi Dravidar and Tribal people will be fully prevented. A dedicated police wing will be set up under the supervision of the Superintendent of Police in each district.
points.title: 6 SC/ST Atrocity Prevention

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Welfare of Adi Dravidars and Tribal Communities

points.text: The TVK government will work with dedication for the social and economic advancement of all people. Therefore, we will urge the Union Government to implement the recommendations of the Sachar Committee. Through this, we will ensure proper representation for minorities in education and employment.
points.title: 1 Sachar Committee Recommendations Will Be Implemented

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 1 Sachar Committee Recommendations Will Be Implemented

points.text: Schemes for minority education will be implemented within a planned time frame. Skill development centres will be set up in every district. Colleges for minority women will be opened through the Wakf Board. The process for obtaining the financial assistance needed to start education and businesses will be simplified.
points.title: 2 Minority Education and Entrepreneurship Contribution

points.text: The TVK government will create legal and policy frameworks to protect minority-run public educational and charitable medical institutions.
points.title: 3 Minority Institutions Will Be Protected

points.text: The TVK government will wage a legal battle in the Supreme Court to permanently strike down the new Waqf Amendment Act.
points.title: 4 Legal Battle Against the Waqf Amendment Act

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 4 Legal Battle Against the Waqf Amendment Act

points.text: The Haj subsidy for Muslims undertaking the Haj pilgrimage will be increased. Subsidies will also be given to Christians undertaking the Jerusalem pilgrimage, and government subsidies will be provided for pilgrimages of all religions.
points.title: 5 Pilgrimage Subsidy for People of All Religions

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 5 Pilgrimage Subsidy for People of All Religions

points.text: Adequate land will be identified and allocated for minority burial grounds (kabarstans) and cemeteries.
points.title: 6 Land for Burial Grounds and Graveyards

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 6 Land for Burial Grounds and Graveyards

points.text: The senior citizen pension will be raised to Rs. 3,000.
points.title: 1 Senior Citizen Pension

points.text: Every year, arrangements will be made to take 2.5 lakh senior citizens on pilgrimages to places of their choice. This will include AC train travel, comfortable accommodation, and good food.
points.title: 2 Free Pilgrimage

points.text: One thousand mobile senior citizens clinics will be started across Tamil Nadu. Each mobile clinic will have a doctor, a nurse, and a physiotherapist. They will visit every village panchayat in Tamil Nadu and perform free medical check-ups. Medicines for chronic illnesses will be provided.
points.title: 3 Vettri Mobile Senior Citizens Clinic

points.text: A financial grant of Rs. 10,000 will be provided for senior citizens to buy essential items such as walking sticks, spectacles, wheelchairs, hearing aids and dentures.
points.title: 4 Senior Citizens Equipment Grant

points.text: Homes for senior citizens will be set up in every district with qualified caregivers, medical staff, recreational facilities and all other amenities they need.
points.title: 5 Senior Citizens' Homes

points.text: Senior citizens' recreation centres will be established with libraries, yoga and meditation classes, and indoor games facilities.
points.title: 6 Senior Citizens' Recreation Centres

points.text: To make use of the experience and skills of senior citizens, their advice will also be sought in government administration.
points.title: 7 Senior Citizens in Governance

points.text: A Senior Citizens Protection Wing will be set up in every District Superintendent of Police's office. A dedicated Nodal Officer will be appointed to resolve issues such as property disputes, harassment and abandonment affecting senior citizens.
points.title: 8 Senior Citizens Protection Wing

points.text: Important government services such as ration card renewal, issuing certificates and others will be provided to senior citizens at their doorstep.
points.title: 9 Doorstep Government Services

points.subsection_title: E. Welfare of Persons with Disabilities
points.text: The monthly pension for persons with disabilities will be increased to Rs. 3,000.
points.title: 1 Increase in Pension

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: E. Welfare of Persons with Disabilities

points.subsection_title: E. Welfare of Persons with Disabilities
points.text: A dedicated special-needs teacher will be appointed in every government school. Similarly, dedicated schools for special children will also be opened.
points.title: 2 Education for Special Children

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: E. Welfare of Persons with Disabilities

points.subsection_title: E. Welfare of Persons with Disabilities
points.text: Financial grants will be provided to persons with disabilities for buying essential equipment such as wheelchairs, spectacles, hearing aids and mobility aids.
points.title: 3 Equipment Grant

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: E. Welfare of Persons with Disabilities

points.subsection_title: E. Welfare of Persons with Disabilities
points.text: We will properly implement the existing 4 percent reservation for persons with disabilities.
points.title: 4 Proper Implementation of Reservation

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: E. Welfare of Persons with Disabilities

points.text: The following schemes will be introduced to protect the welfare of transgender persons: *A consolidated pay of Rs. 3,000 will be given. Skill development training will be provided to help transgender persons find employment. Help will be provided to form self-help groups and to create self-employment opportunities.
points.title: 1 Law to Protect Transgender Welfare and Dignity

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 1 Law to Protect Transgender Welfare and Dignity

points.text: A grant of Rs. 30,000 per year will be given to every weaver family that owns a loom (powerloom/handloom).
points.title: 1 Weavers Grant

points.text: Weavers will be given a 50 percent subsidy for purchasing raw materials such as yarn, dye and chemicals required for the weaving trade.
points.title: 2 Weaving Raw Material Purchase Subsidy

points.text: The free electricity currently provided to homes with handlooms will be increased to 500 units. Similarly, free electricity for homes with powerlooms will be increased to 1,500 units. A 75 percent subsidy will be given to small powerloom enterprises (maximum 12 powerlooms) for installing solar panels.
points.title: 3 Free Electricity for Powerlooms

points.text: All weavers will be provided with life insurance worth Rs. 10 lakh.
points.title: 4 Life Insurance

points.text: The current government contribution to the Tamil Nadu Co-operative Handloom Weavers' Savings and Security Scheme is 8 percent. This amount will be increased to 16 percent.
points.title: 5 Savings and Security Scheme

points.text: Integrated weavers' townships will be developed in the districts of Kanchipuram, Arani, Salem and Erode, which excel in the weaving trade. Solar panels with 3 kW capacity will be installed for the electricity needs of these townships.
points.title: 6 Integrated Weavers' Townships

points.text: Scholarships will be given to weavers' children to pursue higher education in Design and Textile Engineering.
points.title: 7 Scholarship for Weavers' Children

points.text: A dedicated e-commerce brand will be created to sell Tamil Nadu weavers' products directly at the international level. Vettri Thari showrooms will be set up in every major city.
points.title: 8 Vettri Thari

points.text: A 100 percent subsidy will be provided to set up solar panels for loom sheds owned by individuals.
points.title: 9 Solar Panel Subsidy

points.text: The Tamil Nadu Guaranteed Pension Scheme brought in by the DMK government will be abolished, and in line with government employees' demands, the full Old Pension Scheme will be compassionately reviewed and assessed.
points.title: 1 Implementing the Old Pension Scheme

points.text: A Government Employees Welfare Board will be established with an allocation of Rs. 5,000 crore to protect the rights and ensure the welfare of government employees — including government staff, police personnel, school teachers and college professors.
points.title: 2 Government Employees Welfare Board

points.text: Temporary staff appointed on a contract basis in government roles such as teachers and nurses will be made permanent staff after 5 years of service.
points.title: 3 Permanency for Temporary Staff

points.text: It will be ensured that promotions and transfers of government employees take place fairly and transparently.
points.title: 4 Transparency in Promotions and Transfers

points.text: Once announced by the Union Government, the TVK government will immediately implement the 8th Pay Commission in the state to benefit government employees.
points.title: 5 Immediate Implementation of the 8th Pay Commission

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 5 Immediate Implementation of the 8th Pay Commission

points.text: A Police Welfare and Work Reform Act will be brought in and weekly holidays on a rotation basis will be provided. The laundry allowance for police personnel will be raised from Rs. 500 to Rs. 1,000. Police welfare hospitals will be set up in districts including Madurai, Coimbatore, Trichy, Tirunelveli, Salem and Vellore.
points.title: 1 Police Welfare Scheme

points.text: To honour police personnel who act bravely in maintaining law and order and lose their lives, an honorarium of Rs. 1 crore will be given to their families.
points.title: 2 Honorarium for Police Personnel

points.text: The basic monthly pay of police personnel will be increased from the current Rs. 18,200 to Rs. 25,000. Additionally, a monthly Duty Allowance of Rs. 1,000 will be given to police personnel working in divisions with heavy workloads.
points.title: 3 Dignified Pay and Welfare for Police Personnel

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 3 Dignified Pay and Welfare for Police Personnel

points.text: A transparent and accountable police administration free from political interference in transfers and postings will be ensured. All decisions will be taken solely on the basis of administrative needs, performance metrics and professional integrity.
points.title: 4 Transparent and Accountable Police Administration

points.text: The practice of posting police personnel as orderlies in the homes of VIPs in Tamil Nadu against established rules will be stopped.
points.title: 5 Abolition of the Orderly System

points.text: Adequate police quarters will be ensured in all districts so that the families of police personnel on transfer or special duty can stay comfortably.
points.title: 6 Adequate Police Quarters in All Districts

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 6 Adequate Police Quarters in All Districts

points.text: The deployment of women police personnel in bandobust duty will be stopped. It will be ensured that they are posted as front-line police personnel.
points.title: 7 Welfare of Women Police Personnel

points.text: A Tamil Nadu Teachers' Protection Act will be brought in, and teachers working on a temporary basis who have completed 5 years of service will be made permanent teachers. Similarly, during the period they serve as temporary teachers, it will be ensured that they receive 'equal pay for equal work' on par with permanent teachers.
points.title: 1 Equal Work! Equal Pay!

points.text: To improve the qualifications of government school teachers, qualified teachers who are selected will be taken each year to Singapore and Finland — countries that excel in school education — for training.
points.title: 2 Overseas Training for Government School Teachers

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 2 Overseas Training for Government School Teachers

points.text: All vacant posts in government schools will be filled.
points.title: 3 Teacher Recruitment

points.text: An AI and Digital Education Skill Development Scheme will be implemented so that government school teachers can skilfully learn artificial intelligence technology.
points.title: 4 AI Training for Teachers

points.text: The practice of assigning non-teaching duties to government school teachers will be completely stopped.
points.title: 5 Removal of Non-Teaching Duties

points.subsection_title: K. Welfare of Anganwadi and ASHA Workers
points.text: All Anganwadi workers will be made 'Grade D' government employees and paid Rs. 18,000 per month on that basis. Similarly, it will be ensured that Anganwadi helpers and ASHA workers who conduct public-health awareness work also receive a monthly salary of Rs. 10,000.
points.title: 1 Pay Rise

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: K. Welfare of Anganwadi and ASHA Workers

points.subsection_title: K. Welfare of Anganwadi and ASHA Workers
points.text: It will be ensured that Anganwadi and ASHA workers who have completed 20 years of service receive Rs. 15 lakh as retirement benefits.
points.title: 2 Retirement Benefits

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: K. Welfare of Anganwadi and ASHA Workers

points.text: The inhumane practice of human beings manually removing human waste will be completely abolished. Modern machines will be used for this work. Strict non-bailable legal action will be taken against private firms or contractors that violate this.
points.title: 1 Ending Manual Handling of Human Waste

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 1 Ending Manual Handling of Human Waste

points.text: Workers who were previously engaged in manually removing human waste will be trained to operate the modern machines mentioned above and appointed as operators. This will prevent their loss of employment and ensure their dignified livelihood.
points.title: 2 Training for Sanitation Workers

points.text: A monthly pension of Rs. 4,000 will be given to sanitation workers retiring from service.
points.title: 3 Pension Scheme

points.text: A Drivers' Welfare Board will be set up to protect the welfare of registered auto and taxi drivers. They will be provided with life insurance worth Rs. 10 lakh and accident insurance worth Rs. 10 lakh.
points.title: 1 Drivers' Welfare Board

points.text: Procedures for drivers to renew their vehicle permits, driving licences and insurance will be completely digitalised.
points.title: 2 Digital Permits

points.text: The 'Tamil Taxi App' will be introduced in the major cities of Tamil Nadu. No fees such as platform commission will be collected from drivers for this.
points.title: 3 Tamil Taxi App

points.text: To protect the environment, a 50 percent subsidy (up to Rs. 75,000) will be given for newly purchased electric autos. A 50 percent subsidy (up to Rs. 30,000) will be given for converting existing autos to CNG (gas) operation.
points.title: 4 Green Motor Vehicle Scheme

points.text: A Drivers' Day will be declared to honour drivers. On that day, drivers who serve honestly will be given a state government award and a cash prize of Rs. 50,000.
points.title: 5 Best Driver Award

points.text: More than 15 lakh gig workers in Tamil Nadu work independently without any job protection or medical insurance. Considering their welfare, a Tamil Nadu Gig Workers Act will be enacted, ensuring their job security and social status.
points.title: 1 Tamil Nadu Gig Workers Act

points.text: All registered gig workers will be provided with life insurance of Rs. 10 lakh and accident insurance of Rs. 10 lakh.
points.title: 2 Social Security

points.text: Interest-free loans will be given to gig workers employed with firms such as Swiggy and Zomato to buy e-bikes.
points.title: 3 Interest-Free Loans to Buy E-bikes

points.text: Under the Street Vending Regulation and Livelihood Protection Act, special zones for street vending will be set up in every urban local body. Investment loans of up to Rs. 50,000 will be provided interest-free through cooperative banks. Street vendors will only be relocated after alternative locations have been confirmed.
points.title: 1 Act for Regulation of Street Vending and Protection of Livelihoods

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 1 Act for Regulation of Street Vending and Protection of Livelihoods

points.subsection_title: P. Welfare of Journalists and Media Workers
points.text: Life insurance of Rs. 15 lakh will be provided to all accredited journalists. Families of retired journalists with an annual income of less than Rs. 5 lakh will be given a monthly pension of Rs. 15,000. Steps will be taken to accredit local journalists working at the taluk level as accredited journalists.
points.title: Social Security

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: P. Welfare of Journalists and Media Workers

points.subsection_title: Q. Welfare of Lawyers and Legal Advisors
points.text: Lawyers with 20 years of practice experience and an annual income of less than Rs. 5 lakh will be given a pension of Rs. 3,000 per month. Life insurance will be provided to all registered lawyers.
points.title: 1 Tamil Nadu Lawyers' Welfare Fund

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Q. Welfare of Lawyers and Legal Advisors

points.subsection_title: Q. Welfare of Lawyers and Legal Advisors
points.text: To honour legal advisors who provide free legal advice and legal aid to rural people, a monthly honorarium of Rs. 5,000 will be given.
points.title: 2 Honorarium for Legal Advisors

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Q. Welfare of Lawyers and Legal Advisors

points.text: Registered construction workers will be given accident insurance worth Rs. 5 lakh. Restructuring the Construction Workers' Welfare Board: The deficiencies in the welfare board set up for construction workers will be removed, and it will be restructured to function fully.
points.title: Social Security

points.subsection_title: S. Welfare of Palmyra Climbers and Salt Pan Workers
points.text: The palmyra tree, which is Tamil Nadu's state tree and one of the markers of Tamil life, is dwindling. To revive it, the following schemes will be implemented: *The palmyra tree will be declared a protected tree. Further, a Tamil Nadu Palmyra Development Corporation will be established and Rs. 200 crore allocated to it. Using that fund, padaneer processing, palmyra jaggery production and the export of value-added palmyra products will be planned. *During periods when palmyra products are not produced, workers engaged in palmyra-based trades will be given a monthly livelihood support of Rs. 3,000.
points.title: 1 Palmyra Tree Protection

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: S. Welfare of Palmyra Climbers and Salt Pan Workers

points.subsection_title: S. Welfare of Palmyra Climbers and Salt Pan Workers
points.text: The monsoon relief amount given to salt pan workers will be raised to Rs. 7,000.
points.title: 2 Monsoon Relief for Salt Pan Workers

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 2 Monsoon Relief for Salt Pan Workers
S. Welfare of Palmyra Climbers and Salt Pan Workers

points.subsection_title: A. Women's Welfare and Safety Framework
points.text: A dedicated wing for the safety and welfare of adult women and girl children will be set up under the direct supervision of the Chief Minister. Under this wing, all welfare schemes and safety features for women and girls will be introduced, monitored and streamlined.
points.title: 1 Women's Welfare and Safety Setup

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Women's Welfare and Safety Framework

points.text: For every woman head of a family, Rs. 2,500 will be credited to her bank account every month until she turns 60. Through this, women heads of families will move away from depending on others for their basic expenses, and their economic independence will be protected.
points.title: 1 Honoured Women Scheme

points.text: The amount for purchasing 6 gas cylinders per year for each family will be directly credited to their bank account.
points.title: 2 Annapoorani Super Six

points.text: Currently, free travel for women is implemented only on certain government buses. Changing this, the Vettri Payanam scheme — free travel for women — will be implemented on all state-run town, city and inter-district government buses across the state.
points.title: 3 Vettri Payanam (Victory Journey)

points.text: At weddings in families with an annual income of less than Rs. 5 lakh, the bride will be given 8 grams (one sovereign) of gold and a silk saree.
points.title: 4 Annan Seer Scheme

points.text: Every baby born in a government hospital in Tamil Nadu will be given a gold ring and a Maternal Uncle's Gift Kit containing essential items such as soft clothing and diapers.
points.title: 5 Maternal Uncle's Gold Ring Scheme

points.text: Rs. 15,000 per year will be credited in two instalments to the bank account of the mother/guardian of students studying from Class 1 to Class 12 in government and government-aided schools. Through this, students will be prevented from dropping out midway due to financial reasons.
points.title: 6 Kamarajar Education Assurance Scheme

points.text: Poor widows and destitute women will be given a monthly grant of Rs. 3,000.
points.title: 7 Widow and Destitute Women Grant

points.text: Women's self-help groups will be given interest-free loans of up to Rs. 5 lakh. Women's self-help groups that aim to become micro and small entrepreneurs will be given Rs. 5 lakh as a non-repayable investment grant. Women's self-help groups that perform exceptionally well for 5 consecutive years will be given an incentive of Rs. 1 lakh. An e-commerce platform will be created to market products made by women's self-help groups internationally.
points.title: 8 Singappengal Development Scheme

points.text: Food items, linen and school supplies required by government agencies and government service organisations are currently purchased from many private firms. Changing this, 40 percent of such procurement will be made from women's self-help groups and the micro and small enterprises run by them. Through this, stable income for thousands of women entrepreneurs will be ensured.
points.title: 9 Magalir Mahasakthi

points.text: Special industrial estates with infrastructure that fully meets women's needs will be set up in 10 cities: Chennai, Coimbatore, Madurai, Trichy, Salem, Vellore, Tiruppur, Thanjavur, Tuticorin and Tirunelveli.
points.title: 10 Special Industrial Estates

points.text: To enhance women's safety, 500 surveillance teams named after Rani Velu Nachiyar will be set up across Tamil Nadu. Composed entirely of women, these teams will carry out surveillance work in plain clothes with body-worn cameras.
points.title: 1 Rani Velu Nachiyar Force

points.text: The Kaavalan app currently in use for women's safety will be upgraded, and a 'Smart Panic Button' will be installed in all buses, share autos and similar vehicles running across Tamil Nadu. This will be connected to AI technology and monitored 24 hours. CCTV cameras integrated with AI technology will be installed at all major public places. All unsafe areas with low footfall will be identified, and appropriate safety features will be put in place in those areas.
points.title: 2 Integrated Surveillance Centre Powered by AI

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 2 Integrated Surveillance Centre Powered by AI

points.text: Special fast-track courts will be set up to investigate crimes against women and deliver justice quickly.
points.title: 3 Special Fast-Track Courts for Women

points.text: Hostels with proper safety and modern amenities will be set up in major cities across Tamil Nadu for working women and students. The maximum monthly charge for these will be only Rs. 2,500.
points.title: 4 Hostels for Working Women and Students

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 4 Hostels for Working Women and Students

points.text: Pregnant women will be given Rs. 25,000 in financial aid to help them buy nutritious food, carry out monthly medical check-ups and meet essential needs. 'Thaai Care' women's service and help centres will be set up across the state. Pregnant women can stay at these centres and benefit from their services as their delivery date approaches. At these centres, pregnant women will be provided with free food, medical aid and continuous medical monitoring.
points.title: 1 Grant for Pregnant Women

points.text: Free sanitary napkins will be provided to women at ration shops, schools and colleges. Sanitary napkin vending machines will be installed at important locations in every town. Arrangements will be made in all government school toilets to properly dispose of used sanitary napkins.
points.title: 2 Free Sanitary Napkins

points.text: Free HPV vaccines will be given to all girls between the ages of 9 and 14. Additionally, free cervical cancer screening will be done for all women above 30 at primary health centres across Tamil Nadu.
points.title: 3 Free Cervical Cancer Screening and Vaccination

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 3 Free Cervical Cancer Screening and Vaccination

points.text: Free solar stoves will be provided to tribal and hill-dwelling women to reduce the hardships of using firewood stoves.
points.title: 4 Solar Stoves for Tribal Women:

points.text: Unemployed graduates waiting after registering at the employment exchange will be given a monthly grant of Rs. 4,000. Young people who have completed 12th standard or ITI/diploma courses and are waiting for jobs will be given a monthly grant of Rs. 2,500. This grant will be paid for the first two years from the date of registration. This grant will help in their skill development.
points.title: 1 Youth Vettri Fund

points.text: A special law will be enacted to ensure that appointments to government jobs are made fairly and transparently. Through this law, all procedures from the announcement of vacancies to the appointments will be completed within one year.
points.title: 2 Transparency in Employment

points.text: All vacant posts in government departments will be filled swiftly.
points.title: 3 Filling Vacant Posts

points.text: All students studying from UG to PhD will be given collateral-free loans of up to Rs. 20 lakh. Through this, it will be ensured that students can study the course of their choice at the colleges of their choice without any financial barriers.
points.title: 4 Education Guarantee Scheme

points.text: To improve the skills of young people, internship training will be provided in partnership with government and private industrial firms. It will be ensured that 5 lakh youth benefit from this initiative every year. During this training period, graduates will be paid Rs. 10,000 per month and those with vocational education will be paid Rs. 8,000 per month. Priority will be given under this scheme to young people from families without permanent employment.
points.title: 5 Vettri Trade Training

points.text: Young people who wish to start businesses will be given collateral-free loans of up to Rs. 25 lakh by the government. Through this, the dream of becoming an entrepreneur will come true for many young people.
points.title: 6 Vettri Entrepreneur Scheme

points.text: Private firms that employ Tamil Nadu youth up to 75 percent of their workforce will be given a 2.5 percent subsidy on state GST, a 5 percent subsidy on electricity charges, and priority in government procurement. Through this scheme, it will be ensured that Tamil Nadu youth receive extensive employment.
points.title: 7 Local Jobs for Local Youth Scheme

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 7 Local Jobs for Local Youth Scheme

points.text: Under the Chief Minister's People's Service Friend Scheme, 5 lakh young people will be appointed as permanent staff in every village panchayat and urban local body and paid a monthly salary of Rs. 18,000. These staff will ensure that the state government's services and schemes reach every home. This joint initiative between the government and youth will help the administration function better.
points.title: 8 Chief Minister's People's Service Friend Scheme

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 8 Chief Minister's People's Service Friend Scheme

points.text: The following schemes will be implemented to encourage creators who take the greatness of Tamil land's history, art, culture, language, food and music to the world through digital platforms. 1.5 lakh creators will be selected, and their land-based creations will be funded for production costs. 500 creative skill training centres will be set up across Tamil Nadu. Training in video production, digital marketing, content strategy and similar fields will be given at these centres.
points.title: 9 Digital Creators Encouragement Scheme

points.text: To ensure youth participation in Tamil Nadu government policy and administrative decisions, a Tamil Nadu Youth Advisory Council will be created. This council will directly convey their views and suggestions to the Chief Minister and other senior government officials. This council will serve as a bridge between government administration and the thinking of young people.
points.title: 10 Tamil Nadu Youth Advisory Council

points.text: Micro tech parks will be set up in 25 towns including Pollachi, Karur, Kovilpatti, Krishnagiri, Tiruvannamalai, Vellore, Erode, Dindigul, Namakkal and Sivakasi. Each micro tech park will be equipped with infrastructure such as high-speed internet, modern meeting halls and co-working space. These micro tech parks will create opportunities for youth from those towns to work globally while working from their hometowns.
points.title: 11 Micro Tech Parks

points.subsection_title: B. Tamil Nadu Rising in Sports
points.text: A Tamil Nadu Sports University will be set up with high-quality sports training, sports medicine, sports technology, and excellent administrative infrastructure so that Tamil Nadu athletes can win Olympic medals. Sports Excellence Centres will be established to provide training equivalent to that given to athletes in developed countries.
points.title: 1 Tamil Nadu Sports University

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Tamil Nadu Rising in Sports

points.subsection_title: B. Tamil Nadu Rising in Sports
points.text: The Tamil Nadu Olympic Skill Development Scheme will be introduced to make Tamil Nadu the sports capital of India. Through this, talented athletes across Tamil Nadu will be identified and given international-standard training to enable them to win at the Olympics. Individual athletes from Tamil Nadu who qualify for the Olympics will be given Rs. 25 lakh as a grant, and team athletes will be given Rs. 10 lakh. Of these, Tamil Nadu athletes who win gold at the Olympics will be given Rs. 6 crore, silver medallists Rs. 4 crore, and bronze medallists Rs. 3 crore as cash prizes. Similarly, 500 athletes who excel at national and international sports competitions will be given financial assistance.
points.title: 2 Tamil Nadu Vettri Olympic Scheme

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Tamil Nadu Rising in Sports

points.subsection_title: B. Tamil Nadu Rising in Sports
points.text: World-class multi-sport stadiums, sports complexes, gymkhanas, sports academies and training centres will be set up in the 10 zonal capitals of Tamil Nadu.
points.title: 3 World-Class Sports Infrastructure

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Tamil Nadu Rising in Sports

points.subsection_title: B. Tamil Nadu Rising in Sports
points.text: 500 athletes with interest and talent in sports will be identified and selected from schools. Athletes between 8 and 14 years of age thus selected will be given world-class training sufficient to win Olympic medals.
points.title: 4 Young Athletes Identification Scheme

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Tamil Nadu Rising in Sports

points.subsection_title: B. Tamil Nadu Rising in Sports
points.text: Qualified Paralympics athletes will be identified and given world-class training and financial assistance. World-class Paralympics training centres will be set up in Madurai, Salem and Trichy.
points.title: 5 Paralympics Skill Development Scheme

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Tamil Nadu Rising in Sports

points.subsection_title: B. Tamil Nadu Rising in Sports
points.text: (Surfing, Kayaking, Wind-Surfing, Snorkelling, Scuba Diving) 10 water sports centres will be set up. Priority will be given to young coastal fishermen to train at these sports centres.
points.title: 6 Water Sports

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Tamil Nadu Rising in Sports

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: Crop loans taken from cooperative banks by farmers owning less than 5 acres will be fully waived. Fifty percent of crop loans taken from cooperative banks by farmers owning more than 5 acres will be waived.
points.title: 1 Agricultural Loan Waiver

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: Every farmer family that owns agricultural land will be given Rs. 15,000 per year in two instalments (July-August season, January-February season) as seed-season financial aid. This fund will be a support for meeting the expenses of the sowing season.
points.title: 2 Vettri Farmer Entitlement

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: A scheme to issue a Farmer Identity Card to tenant farmers and agricultural labourers will be introduced. Farmers holding this identity card will be directly credited with Rs. 10,000 per year into their bank accounts as a grant.
points.title: 3 Uzhavar Thozhar Scheme

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: Rs. 3,500 per quintal will be given as minimum support price for paddy, and Rs. 450 per tonne for sugarcane. When the market prices of turmeric, sugarcane, paddy and copra fall, the government will also bear that loss. All these funds, including the support price amount and market loss compensation, will be credited directly into farmers' bank accounts.
points.title: 4 Guaranteeing Minimum Support Price for Paddy and Sugarcane

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 4 Guaranteeing Minimum Support Price for Paddy and Sugarcane
Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: A Farmers' Income Enhancement Commission will be established to identify advice on increasing farmers' income, simplified marketing methods and pricing guidance. It will make annual recommendations to the government on these matters.
points.title: 5 Farmers' Income Enhancement Commission

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: To benefit farmers, additional agro-meteorological centres will be established across Tamil Nadu. Through this, crop losses during the rainy seasons will be accurately calculated. Based on this, compensation will be quickly given to farmers. 100 percent of the cultivated land will be brought under crop insurance.
points.title: 6 100 Percent Crop Insurance

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: To ensure that changes in coconut prices do not affect coconut farmers, a copra support fund will be allocated. Through the Coconut Producers' Cooperative Society, copra will be procured at places such as Coimbatore, Thanjavur, Pollachi and Kangeyam. Oil will be extracted from these at government mills and sold through the public distribution system. The Union Government will be strongly urged to introduce Minimum Support Price (MSP) for fresh coconut and, at the same time, to raise the support price for copra (both milling and ball varieties) to protect farmers from price fluctuations and ensure them profitable income. Further, a 'Tamil Nadu Coconut Processing Corporation' will be created to develop value-added products such as neera and coconut oil to increase farmers' income.
points.title: 7 Welfare of Copra Farmers

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: The following steps will be taken to make Tamil Nadu a leading state in organic farming. Farmers switching from synthetic-fertiliser farming to organic farming will be given a monthly grant of Rs. 2,000 for the first three years. A new brand called 'Tamil Iyal', based on geographical indication, will be created to market organically grown traditional rice varieties, spices, coconuts, turmeric and bananas. A Nammalvar College of Organic Agriculture will be established in Thanjavur.
points.title: 8 Tamil Nadu Organic Farming Scheme

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: Farmers joining Farmer Producer Organisations will be given an incentive of Rs. 5,000.
points.title: 9 Farmer Producer Organisation

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: Children of farmers holding less than two acres of agricultural land will be given free higher education.
points.title: 10 Free Higher Education for Farmers' Children

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 10 Free Higher Education for Farmers' Children
Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: Paddy and other procured produce including vegetables, food grains and spices will be properly handled and safeguarded at all procurement centres, cluster hubs and silos. Through this, post-harvest losses will be reduced. Accurate weighing and storage data will be properly maintained. Similarly, it will be ensured that not a single paisa of commission is charged at procurement centres for procurement or for loading and unloading sacks. Whoever charges such commission will face strict action.
points.title: Y Reducing Produce Wastage at Procurement Centres

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Y Reducing Produce Wastage at Procurement Centres
Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: The fishing ban-period relief amount will be raised to Rs. 20,000. Fishermen engaged in traditional fishing will be given Rs. 7,000 as northeast monsoon relief.
points.title: 1 Fishermen Safety Entitlement Amount

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: Every registered fisherman will be given accident insurance worth Rs. 25 lakh. Further, to assist fishermen struggling for their lives in accidents at sea, satellite-technology-equipped sea ambulances will be set up at every major fishing harbour. Tow-boat facilities will also be put in place to rescue disabled boats at sea.
points.title: 2 Fishermen's Accident Insurance

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: The diesel subsidy for fishing boats will be raised from 1,800 litres to 3,600 litres. An additional subsidy of Rs. 15 per litre will be directly credited to the boat owner's bank account.
points.title: 3 Fishermen Fuel Security

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: For the first time in India, Minimum Support Prices will be fixed for prawns, sardines, mackerel and squid. To prevent fishermen from being affected by price crashes when these fish arrive in large quantities, the shortfall amount will be directly credited to their bank accounts.
points.title: 4 Minimum Support Price for Fish

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: To ensure that coasts are free of huts, a coastal housing scheme will be created. Under this scheme, houses will be built to a standard capable of withstanding cyclones, coastal erosion and bad weather. Priority will be given to fishing communities in Nagapattinam, Cuddalore, Ramanathapuram, Tuticorin and Kanyakumari districts — which are most vulnerable to cyclones.
points.title: 5 Sea Mother Home

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: Under the rules currently in force, when a fisherman goes missing at sea, he is officially declared deceased only after 7 years. Only after that is compensation given to his family. Changing this, if a fisherman goes missing, appropriate relief will be given to his family within the next six months.
points.title: 6 Welfare of Missing Fishermen's Families

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: Young fishermen who wish to become entrepreneurs in value-added marine trades such as boat-repair workshops, seafood-export enterprises, fish-processing businesses and seaweed production will be given an investment grant of Rs. 5 lakh.
points.title: 7 Fishermen Entrepreneur Welfare Scheme

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: Fishermen who fish in rivers, tanks, ponds and reservoirs — other than the sea — currently do not receive any of the benefits of the Tamil Nadu Fishermen's Welfare Board. Changing this, all the benefits of the Tamil Nadu Fishermen's Welfare Board will be made available to all fishermen who fish for a living. Eligible prawn farmers will also benefit under this scheme. Further, a prawn disease insurance will be provided to benefit prawn farmers facing losses due to unexpected disease outbreaks.
points.title: 8 Welfare of Inland Fishermen

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: Just as there are Aavin outlets for selling dairy products, dedicated sales outlets will be set up across Tamil Nadu to sell marine food products, to improve the economic condition of fishermen. At these outlets, marine food products such as value-added dried fish and canned fish foods will be sold.
points.title: A Marine Products Sales Outlets

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: A Seaweed Processing Industrial Park will be set up at Rameswaram. Here, seaweed will be turned into food, fertiliser and cosmetics. In this, priority will be given to women from women's self-help groups.
points.title: 10 Seaweed Park

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: Barriers will be built along 200 km in coastal districts most affected by coastal erosion — Nagapattinam, Cuddalore, Viluppuram, Ramanathapuram and Kanyakumari.
points.title: 11 Sea Shield Scheme

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.text: Infrastructure such as cold storage to preserve fish, facilities to convert toxic waste into useful fish feed (Bio Floc Technology), and cage fish farming will be set up at all fishing harbours across Tamil Nadu. A trade chain without middlemen between fishermen and fish traders will be created. Modern processing centres will be set up at all fish-landing sites.
points.title: 12 Development of Fishing Harbours

Through this, groundwater drawn for agriculture will be reduced by 40 percent. The shrinking cultivable area will expand by 30 lakh acres. Crop output will also increase.

points.text: To ensure irrigation security, groundwater recharge and drought mitigation throughout Tamil Nadu, TVK will implement zone-wise water infrastructure projects in mission mode. This will include the Anaimalayaaru-Nallaaru Link Project (Tiruppur/Coimbatore), the Erdhalavu-Pulikarai Link Project (Dharmapuri), the building of check dams and regulators in the Cauvery and Thirumanimuthaaru areas of Namakkal district, the revival of Upper Vaippaaru and Mudangiyaaru rivers (Virudhunagar/Rajapalayam), the Ramanadhi-Jambunadhi Link Canal Project (Tenkasi/Alangulam), the Tamirabarani-Karumeniyaaru-Nambiyaaru Link Project (Tirunelveli/Tuticorin), and other important projects.
points.title: 5 Accelerating Irrigation Projects

Through this, every farmer can fully understand his own land through his smartphone. Drones will be provided at subsidised prices to agricultural cooperative societies for spraying pesticides.

In particular, drone-based pesticide and fertiliser spraying, value-added small milling and processing industries will be encouraged through this.

points.text: Erode turmeric, Krishnagiri mango and copra produced in the Kongu region will be value-added so that Tamil Nadu farmers get higher prices. A turmeric extraction park will be set up in Erode to separate high-grade turmeric for pharmaceutical use from ordinary turmeric. Cargo transport facilities will be arranged to transport mangoes produced in Krishnagiri directly to Chennai or Bengaluru airports for export. A Coconut Value-Addition Centre will be set up in the Kongu region for value addition of coconuts and coconut products.
points.title: A Value-Addition Centres for Turmeric, Mango and Copra!

points.subsection_title: C. Water Management and Agricultural Innovations.
points.text: The Cauvery delta region is the heart of Tamil Nadu agriculture. However, due to environmental degradation, the water resources, soil fertility and agricultural output of the Cauvery delta have suffered major damage. To correct this, the following steps will be taken. Rs. 5,000 crore will be allocated and special attention will be paid to correcting the salinisation of groundwater in the Cauvery delta. Further, steps to raise the depleted groundwater table and measures for soil restoration will be given focused attention. An agricultural ecotoxicology centre will be set up to restore the Cauvery delta; damage to the environment from pollution and chemicals will be investigated and remediated.
points.title: 10 Restoring the Cauvery Delta Ecosystem

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: C. Water Management and Agricultural Innovations.

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.subsection_title: C. Water Management and Agricultural Innovations.
points.text: 'Blue-Green Horticulture Parks' powered by solar electricity will be set up in the coastal districts of Ramanathapuram, Nagapattinam and Tuticorin. Through these, rising salinity in the water in coastal districts will be removed. This water will be used for cash crops like chillies and coriander.
points.title: 11 Coastal Horticulture Scheme

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: C. Water Management and Agricultural Innovations.

points.section_title: Welfare of Farmers, Agricultural Labourers and Fishermen
points.subsection_title: C. Water Management and Agricultural Innovations.
points.text: At every paddy harvest in Tamil Nadu, tonnes of straw are produced. However, much of this is not properly used and is either burnt or disposed of as agricultural waste. Changing this, a scheme to produce biogas from straw will be implemented. For this, 10 straw bio-refineries will be set up in Thanjavur and Tiruvarur districts, and ethanol and biogas will be produced at them. Through this, arrangements will be made for paddy farmers to get additional income. Further, these straw bio-refineries will be managed through local farmer groups.
points.title: 12 Delta Bio-Refinery Centre

points.text: Ramanathapuram and Virudhunagar districts will be declared drought zones. In these drought zones, farmers who grow millets and oilseeds using drip irrigation and sprinkler irrigation methods that use less water will be given an incentive.
points.title: 13 Drought Zones!
