points.text: Every government school in Tamil Nadu will be equipped with world-class science laboratories, computer labs with high-speed internet, STEM-based modern education (Science, Technology, Engineering, Maths), modern toilets, clean drinking water, quality furniture, and counselling centres. By 2031, all school classrooms in Tamil Nadu will be converted into smart classrooms.
points.title: 1 Modernising Government Schools

points.text: Special schemes will be designed and implemented in full force to eliminate gender and social-based disparities in students' learning abilities. Through this, Tamil Nadu will be made the top state in the country in Learning Outcomes, and on par with developed countries.
points.title: 2 Becoming the Top State in Learning Outcomes

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 2 Becoming the Top State in Learning Outcomes

points.text: 100 elite schools will be opened across Tamil Nadu for students from socially and economically disadvantaged families who excel in education. In these schools, education, food, accommodation, textbooks and uniforms will be provided free of cost to students from 6th to 12th standard. Of these 100 schools, 50 will be dedicated exclusively for girl students.
points.title: 3 Kamarajar Special Schools

points.text: To curb the rising school student dropout rate, AI-powered monitoring systems will be established across Tamil Nadu. 1,000 education coordinators will be appointed to identify students dropping out of school due to social and economic reasons and re-admit them to school.
points.title: 4 0% Dropout

points.text: The Chief Minister's Breakfast Scheme will be extended to government schools and government-aided schools up to Class 8, and nutritious breakfast will be provided. Through this scheme, it will be ensured that all students receive balanced nutrition.
points.title: 5 Expansion of the Breakfast Scheme

points.text: The 7.5 percent reservation for government school students in undergraduate degree programmes will be raised to 10 percent. Further, a 10 percent reservation will be given to government school students in postgraduate programmes as well.
points.title: 6 10 Percent Reservation

points.text: A Headmasters' Skill Development Scheme will be implemented to further sharpen the leadership qualities of school headmasters. A dedicated leadership training institute will be set up for this, and continuous training will be given to headmasters across the state. Under this scheme, schools performing well and achieving excellence in learning outcomes will be given Rs. 50 lakh as an incentive.
points.title: 7 Headmasters' Skill Development Scheme

points.text: To encourage entrepreneurship among students from their school days, under the Tamil Nadu Student Entrepreneur Scheme, students studying in Classes 11 and 12 in government schools will be given Rs. 5,100 as investment capital. This fund will help these students develop small-scale business models. Further, special entrepreneurship classes will be conducted as part of extracurricular activities in all schools.
points.title: 8 Tamil Nadu Student Entrepreneur Scheme

points.text: To ensure the mental wellbeing and safety of government school students and teachers, 5,000 mental health counsellors will be appointed, and psychological first aid training will be given to students and teachers. A happiness curriculum will be introduced to reduce stress and boost concentration among students.
points.title: 9 Student Mental Wellbeing Protection

points.text: To turn Tamil Nadu government school students into trail-blazers, artificial intelligence science will be taught in the curriculum from 6th to 12th standard. Further, climate science will also be taught in the curriculum from 6th to 12th standard to help students understand the changing climate and act responsibly.
points.title: 10 AI Module in School Curriculum

points.text: In districts without government professional colleges, five new engineering colleges and three new medical colleges will be opened. Efforts will be made to urge the Union Government to grant approval for the medical colleges. Thirty government arts and science colleges will be upgraded to autonomous colleges.
points.title: 1 New Government Professional Colleges

points.text: Every government school student joining a government college after school will be given Rs. 8,000 to reduce the financial burden of college admission expenses.
points.title: 2 Education for Life Fund

points.text: The government itself will bear the education and living expenses of Tamil Nadu students who qualify to study at the world's top 100 educational institutions. In return, after completing their studies, these students will work for three years as government advisors, or as honorary teachers at Tamil Nadu educational institutions, or as entrepreneurs who invest in Tamil Nadu. This knowledge exchange will help guide successive generations of students.
points.title: 3 First Respect for Intellect

points.text: College fees will be fully waived for first-generation Adi Dravidar and Tribal students who studied in government schools and join government colleges, as well as for first-generation students from economically disadvantaged families.
points.title: 4 Waiver of College Fees for First-Generation Students

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 4 Waiver of College Fees for First-Generation Students

points.text: A dedicated university (Periyar Institute of Emerging Technologies - PIET) will be created for emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence, quantum computing, biotech, climate technology, space technology and cybersecurity. This university will work in partnership with leading universities and technology firms in the world.
points.title: 5 Dedicated University for Emerging Technologies

points.text: ITIs across Tamil Nadu will be modernised and new courses will be added under Augmented Reality (AR), Virtual Reality (VR), Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML). New ITIs will be opened based on student demand.
points.title: 6 Modernisation of ITIs

points.text: Skill training centres for emerging technologies will be set up in every district of Tamil Nadu, and free six-month certificate courses will be taught in areas such as AI, EV technology, animation, cloud computing and cybersecurity. It will be ensured that students get employment in leading Tamil Nadu firms on the basis of these certificate courses.
points.title: 1 Skill Training Centres for Emerging Technologies

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 1 Skill Training Centres for Emerging Technologies

points.text: The following steps will be taken to help rural youth perform well in competitive exams: Every district library will be modernised, and high-quality coaching centres for competitive exams will be set up there. Every young person's learning ability will be analysed through AI, and appropriate guidance will be recommended to them.
points.title: 2 Free Coaching for Competitive Exams

points.subsection_title: A. Industrial Growth - Tamil Nadu's Golden Decade
points.text: A target will be set to raise Tamil Nadu's economy to 1.5 trillion dollars by 2036, and necessary steps will be taken. Through this, it will be ensured that the state sees unprecedented growth over the next ten years.
points.title: 1 1.5 Trillion Dollar Economy

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Industrial Growth - Tamil Nadu's Golden Decade

points.subsection_title: A. Industrial Growth - Tamil Nadu's Golden Decade
points.text: 20 sectors that are leading in industrial growth and have the potential to grow at high speed in future will be selected, and dedicated action plans (Industry Transformation Maps) will be drawn up for them. Through these action plans, industrial growth centres will be set up across the state to ensure government-private joint participation.
points.title: 2 Top 20 Plan

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Industrial Growth - Tamil Nadu's Golden Decade

points.subsection_title: A. Industrial Growth - Tamil Nadu's Golden Decade
points.text: Textiles made in Tiruppur, leather goods produced in Vellore, spare parts manufactured in Chennai, and jewellery made in Erode — products made in Tamil Nadu enjoy worldwide acceptance. Therefore, an EnterpriseTNPlan will be introduced to market all products made in Tamil Nadu to global markets. Under this scheme, large volumes of foreign investment will be attracted. Memoranda of understanding will be signed with multinational firms, and locally made products will be exported. Special export sections will be created in every SIPCOT industrial estate in Tamil Nadu.
points.title: 3 Globalising Tamil Nadu Companies

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Industrial Growth - Tamil Nadu's Golden Decade

points.subsection_title: A. Industrial Growth - Tamil Nadu's Golden Decade
points.text: To make doing business easy and to improve export capacity, the TVK government will comprehensively modernise all SIPCOT industrial parks in Tamil Nadu. Through this, uninterrupted electricity, regular drinking water supply, and strong road infrastructure linking them to cities and ports will be ensured. New SIPCOT parks will be created in backward districts; at the same time, it will be ensured that cultivable land will never be acquired from farmers under any circumstances. All vacant SIPCOT lands will be given priority for manufacturing and commercial use and allotted within a deadline and fully utilised.
points.title: 4 SIPCOT and Industrial Estate Reforms, Modernisation and Land Use

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 4 SIPCOT and Industrial Estate Reforms, Modernisation and Land Use
A. Industrial Growth - Tamil Nadu's Golden Decade

points.subsection_title: B. Revival of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
points.text: Micro, small and medium enterprises in Tamil Nadu are facing hardship due to rising electricity tariffs, improper supply infrastructure and economic pressures. To prevent this, the state will allocate Rs. 15,000 crore to provide loan assistance to MSMEs facing crisis. Loans will be provided to MSMEs from this fund at 4 percent interest.
points.title: 1 Kaapu Loan Assistance Scheme - Rs. 15,000 Crore Loan Assistance

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 1 Kaapu Loan Assistance Scheme - Rs. 15,000 Crore Loan Assistance
B. Revival of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises

points.subsection_title: B. Revival of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
points.text: An immediate ban will be imposed on the rise in fixed charges and peak-hour charges in electricity tariff for MSMEs. Further, electricity tariffs will be rationalised after a comprehensive review. Under the 'Vettri Solar Mission', MSMEs that install rooftop solar panels on their company roofs will be given 100% exemption from electricity tax for five years.
points.title: 2 Electricity Assurance Scheme

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Revival of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises

points.subsection_title: B. Revival of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
points.text: MSMEs in Tamil Nadu are not buying and using technologies in step with the developing world. In particular, they lack the finances to buy AI, sensor technologies and modern production machinery. Because of this, Tamil Nadu's MSMEs are unable to compete in international markets. To avoid this, MSMEs will be given an investment grant or financial aid of up to Rs. 50 lakh to buy and use new technologies. Rs. 1,000 crore will be allocated for this as a Technology Upgradation Fund.
points.title: 3 Technology Upgradation Financial Assistance Scheme

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Revival of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises

points.subsection_title: B. Revival of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
points.text: Forty percent of the goods procured by the government from micro, small and medium industrial firms will be procured from Tamil Nadu-based firms. To facilitate this, a Tamil Nadu Supplier Register will be created to register Tamil Nadu-based micro, small and medium industrial firms. Through this, Tamil Nadu-based firms will be easily identified and given priority in procurement.
points.title: 4 Priority for Tamil Nadu Firms

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Revival of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises

points.text: To improve industrial growth, the difficult procedures for starting industrial firms will be simplified as follows: Micro-industries and cottage industries will be granted licences within 7 working days. Small and medium industries will be granted licences within 15 working days. Mega industries investing more than Rs. 200 crore will be granted licences within 21 working days. If the government does not take any decision within the stated timeframe, the applying firms will be considered to have received the licence automatically.
points.title: 1 Licence in 21 Days

points.text: To ensure that foreign investments from multinational firms flow into Tamil Nadu in large volumes, the Tamil Nadu Investor Facilitation Authority will be created under the Chief Secretary of the Tamil Nadu government. The Industries Secretary will serve as the coordinator of this authority. Firms investing over Rs. 200 crore or providing employment for 5,000 persons will be granted licences within 15 working days through this authority. All subsidies offered by the Tamil Nadu government to industrial investment firms — including electricity subsidies and investment subsidies — will be given on time without any delay.
points.title: 2 Investor Facilitation Authority

points.text: To encourage Non-Resident Tamils to invest in Tamil Nadu, the following steps will be taken: The World Tamils Welfare and Investment Authority will be brought under the direct control of the Chief Minister, and welfare-scheme assistance and investment opportunities for Non-Resident Tamils will be expedited. A Non-Resident Tamils Investment Conference will be held every year. Non-Resident Tamils' welfare branches will be set up in various international cities. To help NRTs invest in their home district, a TIDCO NRI Liaison Officer will be appointed in each of the 38 districts. A Non-Resident Tamils Property Protection Tribunal will be set up.
points.title: 3 Non-Resident Tamils Investment Scheme

points.subsection_title: D. Tamil Nadu as the AI Capital
points.text: By 2035, India's economy is expected to rise by an additional 1.7 trillion US dollars through AI technology. To ensure that Tamils also benefit from this, an AI industry growth plan will be implemented. Under this, 5 lakh young people will be skilled in AI by 2031 through Tamil Nadu's polytechnics, ITIs and engineering colleges. AI Centres of Excellence will be created in 5 areas — medicine, agriculture, manufacturing, Tamil language and climate. An export target of Rs. 35,000 crore by 2031 will be set in AI, Deep-Tech and GCC.
points.title: 1 AI Economic Plan

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: D. Tamil Nadu as the AI Capital

points.subsection_title: D. Tamil Nadu as the AI Capital
points.text: India's first AI Ministry will be established, and a Chief AI Officer will be appointed in every government department on behalf of this ministry. This ministry will take initiatives in AI technology.
points.title: 2 India's First AI Ministry

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: D. Tamil Nadu as the AI Capital

points.subsection_title: D. Tamil Nadu as the AI Capital
points.text: For the first time in India, a government-owned Ultra High Performance GPU Compute Cluster will be created. Tamil Nadu's start-ups and MSMEs can use this facility for AI services free of charge.
points.title: 3 Free AI Services for Tamil Nadu Entrepreneurs

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 3 Free AI Services for Tamil Nadu Entrepreneurs
D. Tamil Nadu as the AI Capital

points.subsection_title: D. Tamil Nadu as the AI Capital
points.text: For the first time in India, a special city will be created for AI under the name 'Arivagam'. This will house an AI University, International Skill Centre, Semiconductor Hardware Testing Lab and modern residential facilities.
points.title: 4 Arivagam AI City

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: D. Tamil Nadu as the AI Capital

points.text: In Tamil Nadu, the per-capita income of developed districts is three times that of backward districts. Changing this, a Balanced Development Budget will be announced. Under this budget, development schemes will be announced for Tamil Nadu's 12 backward districts.
points.title: 1 Backward District Development Budget

points.text: Under this scheme, in consultation with district industry bodies, domain experts and people's representatives, a District Special Industrial Development Scheme will be implemented in all 38 districts. Under this scheme, Rs. 23,000 crore will be spent on district development over 5 years. Apart from Chennai, the Global Investors Meet will be held in rotation in other major cities including Coimbatore, Madurai, Trichy and Salem.
points.title: 2 District Special Industrial Development Scheme

points.text: A Textile Technology Centre and infrastructure upgrade worth Rs. 2,000 crore will be set up in Tiruppur. State-of-the-art robotic machinery centres worth Rs. 1,000 crore will be set up in Coimbatore. Rs. 300 crore will be allocated to upgrade bus/lorry body-building workshops in Karur and Namakkal for EVs. A Design and Commerce Hub will be set up in Erode. A special economic zone worth Rs. 500 crore will be established in Salem. Five integrated mango processing parks will be set up in Dharmapuri and Krishnagiri districts. Pharmaceutical manufacturing zones will be set up in the Hosur-Krishnagiri belt. A Coconut Industrial Park will be set up in Pollachi.
points.title: 3 Western Zone

points.text: A distinct tea brand, Nilgiris Premium, will be created and Rs. 200 crore invested in it. A tea tourism route covering 50 tea estates will be created. Food processing parks will be set up to export Kodaikanal homemade chocolates, Nilgiri Cheese and mountain honey. An Agricultural Technology Research Station worth Rs. 150 crore will be set up in the Nilgiris to help hill farmers.
points.title: 4 Kurinji Zone

points.text: Rs. 500 crore will be allocated for the Tiruppattur-Vellore-Ranipet Leather Industry Development Scheme. Rs. 1,500 crore will be allocated to encourage EV spare parts manufacturing in the Chennai-Hosur region. An Electronic Spare Parts Manufacturing Park worth Rs. 500 crore will be set up in the Sriperumbudur-Oragadam area. The Aerospace Park at Vallam-Vadakal will be upgraded. An internet platform and silk tourism route will be created for the export of Kanchipuram silk. Rs. 500 crore will be allocated to sanitise Cuddalore SIPCOT. Biofuel units will be set up in Viluppuram and Cuddalore districts. EV Battery Testing Labs will be set up at Maraimalainagar SIPCOT. Siruseri SIPCOT IT Park will be transformed into a special centre. A new industrial estate will be created in Kallakurichi.
points.title: 5 Northern Zone

points.text: The Trichy-Thanjavur region will be declared an Aerospace and Heavy Engineering zone. An Aerospace Fabrication Park will be set up near Trichy International Airport. An Agricultural Produce Processing Zone will be set up in Thanjavur. A Granite Design Centre will be created in Pudukottai and Perambalur. A Medical Technology Park worth Rs. 300 crore will be set up in Trichy-Thanjavur. A Building Materials Export Centre will be created in Ariyalur. A fisheries and fish-farming centre will be set up in Mannargudi. A Cauvery Agricultural Produce Processing Centre will be set up in Tiruvarur. Fish Export Centres will be created in Nagapattinam and Mayiladuthurai.
points.title: 6 Central Zone

points.text: Nanguneri Special Economic Zone will be converted to a tax-free trade warehouse zone. The Madurai-Tuticorin rail and industrial corridor will be completed within two years. The Tuticorin-Tirunelveli region will be declared an aerospace industrial investment zone, and a Space Industrial Park will be set up on 2,000 acres. Rs. 400 crore will be allocated for the revival of the Madurai-Virudhunagar weaving industry. The Kanyakumari-Rameswaram coastal region will be declared a Blue Economy Zone. Dindigul lock manufacturers will be organised and given international market opportunities. A cardamom processing centre and SIDCO industrial estate will be set up in Tenkasi. A cardamom value-addition centre will be started in Bodi in Theni district. A tea tourism route and horticulture processing zone will be set up in the hill areas of Theni.
points.title: 7 Southern Zone

points.text: Investment assistance of up to Rs. 50 lakh and business support will be given to 10,000 start-ups in Tamil Nadu over 5 years.
points.title: 1 Start-Smart Tamil Nadu

points.text: World-class incubators will be set up in the districts of Chennai, Coimbatore, Madurai, Trichy and Salem. Through these, free workspace and various advisory services will be provided to entrepreneurs.
points.title: 2 Five World-Class Incubators

points.text: A Venture Capital Fund of Rs. 500 crore will be provided by the government for start-ups in AI, Deep-Tech and FinTech. An independent board will be set up to manage this.
points.title: 3 Venture Capital Fund

points.text: A Tamil Nadu Start-up Summit will be held every year to bring together start-up founders and investors from around the world.
points.title: 4 Tamil Nadu Start-up Summit

points.subsection_title: G. Tamil Nadu as the Guiding Light
points.text: 20,000 Fast Charging stations will be set up by 2031. A 20 percent cash subsidy will be given to battery cell manufacturers. PLI Scheme benefits will be given to lithium and cobalt recycling plants. Traditional mechanics will be given Rs. 10,000 in financial aid to retrain as EV mechanics. A 50 percent subsidy will be given for EV charging infrastructure in apartment complexes. Commercial EV vehicles will be given 100 percent motor vehicle tax exemption for 5 years.
points.title: 1 EV Vehicles and Battery Production

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: G. Tamil Nadu as the Guiding Light

points.subsection_title: G. Tamil Nadu as the Guiding Light
points.text: Special commercial drone corridors will be set up in 10 districts of Tamil Nadu. No police permission will be required for drone operations on these corridors. The use of locally manufactured drones will be made mandatory.
points.title: 2 India's First Commercial Drone Corridor

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: G. Tamil Nadu as the Guiding Light

points.subsection_title: G. Tamil Nadu as the Guiding Light
points.text: The i-Tamil FinTech Corridor will be set up in Coimbatore. 30 percent of Cloud Server costs will be reimbursed to FinTech start-ups in the first three years.
points.title: 3 India's First Digital Finance Hub

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: G. Tamil Nadu as the Guiding Light

points.subsection_title: G. Tamil Nadu as the Guiding Light
points.text: A Creative Economy Policy for animation, gaming and the like will be designed. A 25 percent government subsidy will be given for creations produced in Tamil Nadu. AVGC centres will be set up in 500 government schools. Digital creators will be regularised like MSMEs. Training centres and an IP Protection Fund will be created for gaming start-ups. Five years of state GST will be refunded to VFX studios.
points.title: 4 Tamil Nadu as a Global Content Hub; AVGC-XR Creative Economy

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 4 Tamil Nadu as a Global Content Hub; AVGC-XR Creative Economy
G. Tamil Nadu as the Guiding Light

points.subsection_title: G. Tamil Nadu as the Guiding Light
points.text: The Tuticorin-Tirunelveli region will be turned into a Space Technology Special Investment Region with tax exemptions. Start-ups will be encouraged with Rs. 100 crore through the Sky Start Seed Fund. Space testing centres will be built by the government. Government funds will be allocated for satellites designed by students.
points.title: 5 Space Technology; Kulasekarapattinam Space Research Investment

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 5 Space Technology; Kulasekarapattinam Space Research Investment
G. Tamil Nadu as the Guiding Light

points.subsection_title: G. Tamil Nadu as the Guiding Light
points.text: Tuticorin will be transformed into South Asia's premier port and a Green Ammonia bunkering hub for international ships. Investors will be given incentives and subsidies for ten years.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 6 Green Hydrogen Hub at Tuticorin Port
G. Tamil Nadu as the Guiding Light

points.subsection_title: A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family
points.text: Ordinary families are forced to spend their life savings on quality medical care. Once the TVK government is formed, a 'People's Welfare Insurance' card worth Rs. 25 lakh will be provided to every family. Treatment for all kinds of medical services — including cancer, heart surgery, dialysis and robotic surgery — can be availed through this insurance.
points.title: 1 People's Welfare Protection; Rs. 25 Lakh Medical Insurance for Every Family

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 1 People's Welfare Protection; Rs. 25 Lakh Medical Insurance for Every Family
A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family

points.subsection_title: A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family
points.text: Each person in Tamil Nadu will be given a free full body check-up once a year. Through this, people will be able to monitor their health and take precautions against diseases such as cancer and diabetes.
points.title: 2 Free Full Body Check-up

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family

points.subsection_title: A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family
points.text: Five backward areas, starting in the southern part of Tamil Nadu, will be selected, and five multi-speciality hospitals will be opened with 500 beds each, digital ICUs, and modern diagnostic facilities.
points.title: 3 Five New Multi-Speciality Hospitals

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family

points.subsection_title: A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family
points.text: All Primary Health Centres will be upgraded to the 'IPHS 2025' standard. Medical staff including three doctors, a lab technician and a mental health counsellor will be on duty 24x7.
points.title: 4 Modern Primary Health Centres

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family

points.subsection_title: A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family
points.text: 3,000 People's Welfare Stations will be set up with a nurse, telemedicine facilities to consult with the nearby Primary Health Centre, and diagnostic equipment. These People's Welfare Stations will operate from 7 am to 9 pm, all seven days a week.
points.title: 5 People's Welfare Station; 3,000 Clinics with Telemedicine

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 5 People's Welfare Station; 3,000 Clinics with Telemedicine
A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family

points.subsection_title: A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family
points.text: Free IVF (artificial insemination) centres will be set up in all government district headquarters hospitals and government medical college hospitals across Tamil Nadu. Through this, all treatments related to IVF will be provided free of cost.
points.title: 6 IVF Santhathi Scheme; Treatment for Childless Couples

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 6 IVF Santhathi Scheme; Treatment for Childless Couples
A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family

points.subsection_title: A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family
points.text: All rural hospitals will be modernised with a two-bed dialysis unit, a four-bed emergency care unit, maternity examination and operation theatre, and an increased fleet of rapid ambulances.
points.title: 7 Modernising Rural Hospitals

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family

points.subsection_title: A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family
points.text: High-cost premium medicines will be available in all government hospitals and medical college hospitals at 50 to 90 percent below market prices.
points.title: 8 Medicines at Lower Prices

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family

points.subsection_title: A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family
points.text: The TVK government will create a state-wide 'Happiness Ecosystem' in partnership with large corporations, community organisations and educational institutions to improve mental wellness, social harmony and quality of life. Through measurable outcomes and people-centred administration, TVK will position Tamil Nadu as the best state in the country for citizen wellbeing and happiness. This will become a national model for compassionate governance.
points.title: 9 Happy Tamil Nadu Mission

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family

points.subsection_title: A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family
points.text: Mental healthcare is a largely overlooked medical crisis in Tamil Nadu today. A 10-bed mental health counselling medical unit will be opened with each community hospital. A mental health counsellor will be posted at every high school. Rs. 500 crore will be allocated annually in the budget for mental healthcare.
points.title: 10 Mental Health Scheme

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family

points.subsection_title: A. World-Class Healthcare for Every Family
points.text: The TVK government will frame a Siddha Wellness Tourism Scheme. A government-recognised Siddha Rehabilitation Certificate will be issued. Siddha centres will be set up in Nilgiris, Kodaikanal, Yercaud and Courtallam. These will be integrated with Siddha tourism cities such as Tiruvannamalai, Chidambaram and Courtallam.
points.title: 11 Tamil Nadu as a World-Famous Siddha Identity

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 11 Tamil Nadu as a World-Famous Siddha Identity

points.subsection_title: B. Safeguarding Tamil Nadu's Future; Concerns on Environment, Forests and Climate Change
points.text: To help Tamil Nadu excel in the measures against climate change identified by the UN, Rs. 3,000 crore will be allocated as a Climate Budget. These funds — which cannot be used for anything else — will be spent only on moving the state towards renewable energy and attracting green investment.
points.title: 1 Tamil Nadu Climate Budget - Rs. 3,000 Crore

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 1 Tamil Nadu Climate Budget - Rs. 3,000 Crore
B. Safeguarding Tamil Nadu's Future; Concerns on Environment, Forests and Climate Change

points.subsection_title: B. Safeguarding Tamil Nadu's Future; Concerns on Environment, Forests and Climate Change
points.text: Many lives are being lost because proper preventive action is not being taken against environmental disasters such as cyclones, floods and heat waves. Integrated disaster management offices will be created in all 38 districts, flood-prevention infrastructure in 5 coastal cities including Chennai, and dedicated disaster management offices for heat waves in all 38 districts — handling everything from local heat wave prediction to immediate medical examination.
points.title: 2 Disaster Management that Prevents Deaths

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Safeguarding Tamil Nadu's Future; Concerns on Environment, Forests and Climate Change

points.subsection_title: B. Safeguarding Tamil Nadu's Future; Concerns on Environment, Forests and Climate Change
points.text: A Sustainable Energy Economy Wing will be created directly under the Chief Minister's Office. A plan will be drawn up with fund allocation for climate-protection activities — issuing green bonds to grow the sustainable-energy economy, allocating funds for new innovations linked to climate protection, restoring water resources and controlling carbon emissions.
points.title: 3 Sustainable Energy Economy Wing

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Safeguarding Tamil Nadu's Future; Concerns on Environment, Forests and Climate Change

points.subsection_title: B. Safeguarding Tamil Nadu's Future; Concerns on Environment, Forests and Climate Change
points.text: The TVK government is committed to increasing Tamil Nadu's forest cover to 33% by 2031. In partnership with tribal communities, the restoration of sandalwood trees in the Eastern Ghats and the economic opportunities arising from them will be regulated. Local fauna more closely linked to the forests will be reintroduced in place of foreign animals that are not connected to them.
points.title: 4 Green Tamil Nadu Scheme; 33% Forests in Five Years

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 4 Green Tamil Nadu Scheme; 33% Forests in Five Years
B. Safeguarding Tamil Nadu's Future; Concerns on Environment, Forests and Climate Change

points.subsection_title: B. Safeguarding Tamil Nadu's Future; Concerns on Environment, Forests and Climate Change
points.text: Because successive governments have not enforced proper climate-related controls on industries, Tamil Nadu's three major rivers — the Cauvery, Vaigai and Tamirabarani — have been suffering destruction. Under the TVK government, this will be firmly stopped. In areas such as Erode and Tiruppur, the discharge of textile waste into rivers will be completely stopped, and attention will be given to alternative arrangements. A Cauvery riverfront park will be built in Trichy, a Vaigai riverfront park in Madurai, and a Tamirabarani riverfront park in Tirunelveli.
points.title: 5 Clean Rivers

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Safeguarding Tamil Nadu's Future; Concerns on Environment, Forests and Climate Change

points.subsection_title: B. Safeguarding Tamil Nadu's Future; Concerns on Environment, Forests and Climate Change
points.text: In the forests of Nilgiris, Coimbatore, Dharmapuri, Krishnagiri, Salem and Erode districts, a 50 km solar-powered energy line and 500 forest-protection officers with GPS-connected vehicles will be on patrol at night for the safety of tribal people. Rs. 10 lakh relief will be given to families of people killed by wild animals, and Rs. 5 lakh to those seriously injured. Compensation for crop losses will be given immediately.
points.title: 6 Forests and People

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Safeguarding Tamil Nadu's Future; Concerns on Environment, Forests and Climate Change

points.subsection_title: B. Safeguarding Tamil Nadu's Future; Concerns on Environment, Forests and Climate Change
points.text: Mangrove forests were a major factor in saving many people from the 2004 tsunami. These will be linked with the Forest Department and protected as a conservation zone.
points.title: 7 Mangrove Forest Protection

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Safeguarding Tamil Nadu's Future; Concerns on Environment, Forests and Climate Change

points.subsection_title: B. Safeguarding Tamil Nadu's Future; Concerns on Environment, Forests and Climate Change
points.text: Tamil Nadu's air pollution levels often reach dangerous levels. To control air pollution, Rs. 500 crore will be allocated annually, and schemes will be drawn up and monitored.
points.title: 3 Clean Air

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Safeguarding Tamil Nadu's Future; Concerns on Environment, Forests and Climate Change

points.subsection_title: B. Safeguarding Tamil Nadu's Future; Concerns on Environment, Forests and Climate Change
points.text: Chennai's Cooum and Adyar rivers will be restored through a Mission-mode approach to ensure ecological revival and pollution control.
points.title: 9 Cooum and Adyar Rivers Revival Mission

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 9 Cooum and Adyar Rivers Revival Mission
B. Safeguarding Tamil Nadu's Future; Concerns on Environment, Forests and Climate Change

points.subsection_title: B. Safeguarding Tamil Nadu's Future; Concerns on Environment, Forests and Climate Change
points.text: Encroachments on the Pallikaranai marshland will be removed; further, this marshland will be protected and restored as an important urban wetland and biodiversity zone.
points.title: 10 Pallikaranai Marshland Protection and Restoration

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: B. Safeguarding Tamil Nadu's Future; Concerns on Environment, Forests and Climate Change

points.text: As of today, there is an unannounced power-cut crisis of 2 to 8 hours in Tamil Nadu's villages. Under TVK rule, 24-hour uninterrupted electricity will be ensured. Through production carried out with 10,000 overloaded-feeder connections, TANGEDCO will achieve an outage-free state.
points.title: 1 24-Hour Uninterrupted Electricity

points.text: By installing solar panels on every home's rooftop, our state will become self-sufficient in electricity. The TVK government will give subsidies to those switching to solar electricity at the rate of Rs. 35,000 for 1kW, Rs. 70,000 for 2kW and Rs. 1 lakh for 3kW+.
points.title: 2 Solar Subsidy; Rs. 35,000 for 1kW, Rs. 70,000 for 2kW, Rs. 1 Lakh for 3kW+

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 2 Solar Subsidy; Rs. 35,000 for 1kW, Rs. 70,000 for 2kW, Rs. 1 Lakh for 3kW+

points.text: The current bi-monthly electricity billing will be removed, and monthly electricity billing will be implemented.
points.title: 3 Monthly Electricity Billing

points.text: Due to TANGEDCO's inefficient power distribution and theft, up to 16% of electricity is lost. This amounts to about Rs. 3,000 crore per year. To prevent this, the TVK government will bring in a five-year reform scheme. Through this, 1,000 old transformers will be replaced; by reducing AT&C losses from 16% to 10%, introducing time-of-use tariff systems, and charging residential tariffs for apartment complex common areas (instead of the commercial tariffs currently charged), electricity bills for 30 lakh urban apartment residents will be reduced.
points.title: 4 TANGEDCO Reform; Reducing Electricity Charges for Apartment Residents and MSMEs

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 4 TANGEDCO Reform; Reducing Electricity Charges for Apartment Residents and MSMEs

points.text: A target will be set and executed to source 50% of Tamil Nadu's consumption from renewable energy by 2031. To control urban air pollution, energy costs and carbon emissions, all 5,000 buses operating under TNSTC will be fully electrified.
points.title: 5 50% Renewable Energy Use and Full Electrification of Buses by 2031

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 5 50% Renewable Energy Use and Full Electrification of Buses by 2031

points.text: The TVK government will quickly complete the 118.9 km Chennai Metro Rail Phase 2 and start and complete the 40 km Coimbatore Metro Rail Phase 1. Within 18 months of taking office, the Detailed Project Report for the Madurai Metro Rail Project will be submitted. Electric auto services will be provided from every metro station to the nearest major junctions.
points.title: 1 Metro Expansion; Chennai Metro Phase 2, Coimbatore Metro Phase 1 and Madurai Metro Project Draft

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 1 Metro Expansion; Chennai Metro Phase 2, Coimbatore Metro Phase 1 and Madurai Metro Project Draft

points.text: The Union Government will be urged to provide night flight services at Tuticorin airport, to set up a dedicated cargo-transfer terminal, and to expand Coimbatore airport to international standards through a public-private partnership.
points.title: 2 Night Flight Services and Cargo Terminal at Tuticorin Airport, and PPP Expansion of Coimbatore Airport

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 2 Night Flight Services and Cargo Terminal at Tuticorin Airport, and PPP Expansion of Coimbatore Airport

points.text: Railway link lines in Tamil Nadu have been continuously neglected by successive Union Governments. The Tindivanam-Tiruvannamalai line, the Tuticorin-Madurai line via Aruppukottai, and the fourth line between Tambaram and Chengalpattu were announced but have not yet been allocated funds. The TVK government will prepare a 'Tamil Nadu Rail Link Line Scheme' and urge the Union Government to implement and complete the scheme within a specified timeframe.
points.title: 3 Rail Link Line Scheme

points.text: The TVK government will take steps to fully operationalise the Enayam transshipment port in Kanyakumari district, which has been pending for eight years, and to upgrade the Cuddalore port as a new industrial port.
points.title: 4 Ports

points.text: Multi-modal logistics parks will be set up at three locations that consolidate freight transport and freight security operations: the Chennai port-rail corridor area, the Coimbatore industrial corridor in the west, and Tuticorin in the south. Through this, export costs for Tamil Nadu traders will come down by 20% to 30%, and Tamil Nadu will grow into the premier trade transit hub in Southeast Asia.
points.title: 5 Multi-Modal Logistics Park

points.text: On Tamil Nadu's highways, traffic violations and accidents will be prevented through technological surveillance and AI-assisted speed-control cameras in accident-prone areas. Individuals who help accident victims — autos, cabs and private vehicle owners — will be given financial incentives and presented with a Good Samaritan Life-Saver Award in recognition.
points.title: 6 Technology-Enabled Roads and Accident-Free Transport

points.text: Currently Rs. 20,000 crore is allocated annually for Tamil Nadu highways, district roads and village roads. Yet roads continue to be potholed, causing two-wheeler accidents. Under the TVK government, Rs. 2,000 crore will be spent annually on immediately repairing road damage. Through a government-introduced app, citizens can immediately report road defects with photographs.
points.title: 7 Damage-Free Highways

points.text: To revive the maritime journeys of ancient Tamils, the TVK government will make the necessary arrangements to start maritime passenger services from Nagai port to Colombo and Kuala Lumpur.
points.title: 3 Shipping Services from Nagapattinam to Sri Lanka and Malaysia

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 3 Shipping Services from Nagapattinam to Sri Lanka and Malaysia

points.text: Over the next five years, all panchayats will be connected to the highways. Roads will connect every place — schools, primary health centres, and important locations — in a comprehensive manner.
points.title: 1 Roads Connecting All Panchayats

points.text: The rural employment guarantee scheme will be increased to 150 days, and efforts will be made to fix appropriate wages.
points.title: 2 Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme to be Increased to 150 Days

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 2 Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme to be Increased to 150 Days

points.text: As part of the Tamil Nadu Integrated Development Scheme, 5,000 villages will be taken up as models initially, and urban-grade modern infrastructure will be developed there. All water bodies will be rejuvenated, and sports grounds, parks, community halls, street lighting on all streets, door-to-door solid waste collection, and village forests using natural resources that are the identity of each village will be created. Model villages will be selected every year during Pongal. A development prize of Rs. 50 lakh will be given to the best-performing village.
points.title: 3 Model Villages; Urban-Grade Modern Infrastructure for 5,000 Villages

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 3 Model Villages; Urban-Grade Modern Infrastructure for 5,000 Villages

points.text: The TVK government will create 1,000 Village Industry Clusters with integrated infrastructure including industrial sheds, electricity, drinking water, roads, logistics, storage warehouses, digital connectivity and basic amenities for workers. These clusters will serve as self-sustaining rural economic hubs and will attract MSMEs and start-ups. Through this, lakhs of jobs will be created, and balanced regional development will be ensured.
points.title: 4 Rural Industrial Infrastructure Scheme

points.text: Both permanent and weekly markets in every village will be upgraded with modern infrastructure. The markets will operate with weighing machines, price display boards, cold-storage units to preserve fresh produce, direct links with wholesale traders in cities, and integration with government digital sales platforms.
points.title: 5 Village Markets

points.text: By 2031, proper housing, drainage management and proper housing titles will be handed over to everyone, and the goal of slum-free cities will be achieved.
points.title: 1 Slum-Free Cities by 2031

points.text: Special schemes will be drawn up to protect and enhance the pride of the heritage cities of Thanjavur, Madurai, Kanchipuram and Mahabalipuram — including structure-sensitive renewal, drainage restoration, special training for local artists and craftspeople, and cultural tourism that lifts the local economy. For this, a 'Tamil Nadu Heritage Cities Authority' will be created.
points.title: 2 Heritage Cities Scheme; Protecting Heritage Cities like Thanjavur, Madurai, Kanchipuram and Mahabalipuram

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 2 Heritage Cities Scheme; Protecting Heritage Cities like Thanjavur, Madurai, Kanchipuram and Mahabalipuram

points.text: The TVK government will develop a realistic 'Climate-Resilient Master Plan Draft 2045' that is sustainable and climate-resilient for all districts. Remaining water bodies and natural ecosystems will be identified, recovered and restored, and strict action will be taken against water-body encroachments.
points.title: 3 Climate-Resilient Tamil Nadu Master Plan 2045

points.text: Improved stormwater drains will be installed in flood-risk cities including Chennai. In all municipal corporations, at least 3% of land will be preserved as marshland so that rainwater does not accumulate as floods but is protected underground.
points.title: 4 Chennai, Tuticorin, Cuddalore, Nagai and Kanyakumari Will Be Protected from Flood Risk

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 4 Chennai, Tuticorin, Cuddalore, Nagai and Kanyakumari Will Be Protected from Flood Risk

points.text: The cities of Chennai, Trichy, Madurai and Coimbatore will be developed to world-class standards with long-term future plans, planned growth to address housing pressure, modern infrastructure, and improved quality of life.
points.title: Chennai, Trichy, Madurai, Coimbatore as World-Class Cities

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Chennai, Trichy, Madurai, Coimbatore as World-Class Cities

points.text: With government and private participation, various types of modern parking facilities will be introduced in major cities.
points.title: Varied Parking Options

points.text: In 25 municipal corporations, streets will be developed as modern, people-friendly streets directly connected to roads, with proper pavements, dedicated bicycle lanes, proper connectivity at cross-streets, and modern LED street lighting.
points.title: Modern Streets for the People

points.text: The TVK government will build about 1.5 lakh public toilets covering a total of 1 lakh kilometres on both sides of city roads. Through this, it will be ensured that toilet facilities are available within 500 metres to 1 kilometre. Further, safe and hygienic toilet facilities will be set up at all petrol stations, bus stations, metro and railway stations.
points.title: 3 Public Toilet Infrastructure Scheme

points.text: To reduce the steep property tax increases imposed by the DMK government, the TVK government will introduce a new and fair property tax framework. This will be implemented with the aim of reducing the tax burden on residential property owners.
points.title: A Property Tax Reduction

points.section_title: People's Government; Honest and Upright Administration
points.subsection_title: A. A 21st Century Government: Our administration will be built on honesty, accountability, transparency and putting people's welfare first.
points.text: Every government scheme of ours will reach the people directly and certainly. In that vein, our government will be the first in India to deliver welfare schemes directly to the people without forcibly demanding documents (No-Application). Today, for people to receive government schemes, they must fulfil eligibility criteria, properly fill out applications, contact the relevant office, and even pay bribes to officials. This is an unfair system that deceives people, not welfare-oriented governance. In this context, within the next five to seven years, our TVK government will advance Tamil Nadu to be the first state in India 'to implement welfare schemes without forcibly demanding any documents from the people (No-Application)'. With the help of technology, through digital information including Aadhaar, ration card, family details and family activities, we will identify the persons eligible for welfare schemes. Eligible beneficiaries will receive the benefits of schemes without any application procedure. Through the 'Chief Minister's People's Service Friend', the benefits of schemes will be delivered to beneficiaries at their doorstep. [cite: 3068, 3069, 3070, 3071, 3072, 3073, 3074, 3075]
points.title: 1 No-Application Scheme

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. A 21st Century Government: Our administration will be built on honesty, accountability, transparency and putting people's welfare first.
People's Government; Honest and Upright Administration

points.subsection_title: A. A 21st Century Government: Our administration will be built on honesty, accountability, transparency and putting people's welfare first.
points.text: Like the Aadhaar card, an Entitlement Card will be issued to every family. Through this, eligible families can easily receive the benefits of all their services. This will ensure that our government administration is fair to all, with no room for political interference. [cite: 3086, 3087]

points.subsection_title: A. A 21st Century Government: Our administration will be built on honesty, accountability, transparency and putting people's welfare first.
points.text: The TVK government will launch the 'Tamil Nadu Governance Monitoring Platform' (Real Time Governance Dashboard) as an online information system. Through this, the government's revenue and expenditure accounts, the amount spent per constituency and per district, public complaints and questions related to these, and the government's responses — all of these will be uploaded live in real time. [cite: 3089, 3090]
points.title: 5 Tamil Nadu Governance Monitoring Platform

points.subsection_title: B. Citizens as the Government's Guides
points.text: To enable people to convey their demands directly to the government, bypassing their legislator, a digital platform called 'Makkal Arangam' will be created. Through this, people can directly raise their complaints. *Demands signed and uploaded jointly by 10,000 people will be taken up for government consideration. *Demands signed and uploaded by 5 lakh people will be debated in the Legislative Assembly as a one-day 'Makkal Arangam' discussion. [cite: 3093, 3094, 3095, 3096]

points.subsection_title: B. Citizens as the Government's Guides
points.text: In all districts, people will decide which schemes 10% of the development funds allocated to the district should be used for. During the budget, this will be separately categorised as a 'Participatory Budget' and schemes will be implemented. In each district, a committee of 10 persons — consisting of women, youth, Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe people, and domain experts — will be formed. Their job will be to monitor and evaluate district expenditure and to convey people's demands to the government. [cite: 3104, 3105, 3106, 3107]

points.section_title: People's Government; Honest and Upright Administration
points.text: Because people are unable to complain boldly against bribery, corruption continues to flourish. Against this, under TVK rule, complaint numbers will be displayed in the central area of every government office. If people report the practices of officials demanding bribes to this number — which is linked to the Chief Minister's office — whistleblowers will not only be given appropriate recognition, they will also be protected under the State Whistleblower Protection Act. [cite: 3111, 3112]
points.title: 1 Anti-Corruption People's Helpline

points.section_title: People's Government; Honest and Upright Administration
points.text: An Anti-Narcotics Force will be created under the direct supervision of the Chief Minister. Through this, by operating on a war footing, drug use will be completely eradicated. Those selling narcotics will not only be arrested but will also be booked under the Goondas Act. Anti-Drug Clubs will be set up in every school. The TVK government will create a 'Rehabilitation Fund' to help people recovering from addiction. Further, villages that eradicate drug abuse will be given a special fund of Rs. 10 lakh for infrastructure projects, along with a government evaluation certificate. [cite: 3127, 3128, 3129, 3130]
points.title: 1 Drug-Free Tamil Nadu

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: People's Government; Honest and Upright Administration

points.section_title: People's Government; Honest and Upright Administration
points.text: The TVK government will follow a steady and graduated approach to reduce alcohol consumption, with the primary aim of protecting the welfare of women, families and homes. The TVK government will work to reduce the social and financial burdens caused by alcohol through a controlled reduction in alcohol availability, strong de-addiction programmes, and awareness led by women's groups. In particular, it will be ensured that TASMAC shops located near schools and temples are immediately closed or relocated, and a safe environment will be restored. [cite: 3132, 3133, 3134]
points.title: 2 Healthy, Drug-Free Tamil Nadu

points.text: Every custodial killing that happens in a state is proof of the failure of the government administering that state. Under our TVK rule, independent inquiries will be conducted to secure justice for the families affected by custodial deaths that took place during DMK rule. Strict action will be taken against all those involved in such brutal violence, without fear or favour. [cite: 3140, 3141, 3142]

points.subsection_title: E. Five Special Scheme Budget Statements
points.text: Under this budget, the announcement of funds for the schemes, their reach to beneficiaries, and the resulting social impact will be subjected to intense monitoring and review. They are: *Social Justice Budget: Funds allocated for the equitable advancement of women, Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes, and all marginalised people will be spent entirely without any diversion. *Balanced Regional Development Budget: Impartial development schemes will be drawn up for all backward districts including the 12 most backward ones. *Tamil Nadu Art and Heritage Budget: Funds for recovering and preserving excavation sites, Keezhadi excavations, and taking Tamil culture to the world stage. *Education and Healthcare Equal Opportunity Budget: Funds to close the quality gap between educational institutions and hospitals, to fill vacancies, and to provide world-class education and healthcare. *Ecological Budget: Protection of marine resources, greening of dry districts, flood-prevention management in cities, new agricultural practices, and addressing climate change. [cite: 3151, 3152, 3157, 3158, 3159, 3160, 3161, 3162]

points.subsection_title: F. Economic Sovereignty: We will grow revenue, prevent losses, and build new resources.
points.text: In the 2026-27 financial year, Tamil Nadu's debt exceeds Rs. 10.71 lakh crore. The TVK government's functioning will not waste money meant to be spent on the people. Without imposing new taxes on the people of Tamil Nadu, it will prevent losses, grow revenue and build economic infrastructure in new ways. The TVK government will pursue revenue growth through modern methods — eradicating corruption, mobilising Non-Resident Tamils to attract new investments, reviving micro and small industries, and employing technology, ecology and new-knowledge efficiency. [cite: 3166, 3169, 3170, 3175, 3176]
points.title: 1 We Will Transform Tamil Nadu into an Economically Sovereign State.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 1 We Will Transform Tamil Nadu into an Economically Sovereign State.

points.section_title: People's Government; Honest and Upright Administration
points.subsection_title: F. Economic Sovereignty: We will grow revenue, prevent losses, and build new resources.
points.text: Moving away from relying only on taxes and subsidies, the TVK government will take Tamil Nadu towards a new wealth-creating economic model. The TVK government will set up a Strategic Investment Authority with a valuation of Rs. 50,000 crore. Through this, the government will invest directly as a stakeholder in high-growth sectors such as semiconductors, electric vehicles (EVs), aerospace, artificial intelligence (AI) and advanced manufacturing. Rather than merely facilitating investments, under TVK rule the Tamil Nadu government will jointly invest with industry, create new values, and share their ownership. This will create lakhs of high-quality jobs and generate new non-tax revenue sources for the state. [cite: 3178, 3179, 3180, 3181, 3182]
points.title: 2 Tamil Nadu Strategic Investment Authority (TNSIA)
